description: It's a Long Way to the Top is bagpipes meeting electric guitar. The Jack is a blues song about venereal disease. Australia's finest export.
Bon Scott's last ride. The title track is perfect rock. Touch Too Much and Shot Down in Flames hit just as hard.
The mourning album that became the party album. Hells Bells opens with a funeral toll. You Shook Me All Night Long closes the argument.
The title track is eight minutes of Angus losing his mind. Whole Lotta Rosie is the anthem. Bad Boy Boogie doesn't quit.
My Baby Just Cares for Me became a hit 30 years later. Plain Gold Ring shows classical training meeting jazz instinct. Arrived fully formed.
Sinnerman is ten minutes of relentless piano and percussion. Strange Fruit from a Black woman's mouth hits different. A masterpiece.

album_title: I Put a Spell on You
description: Feeling Good became the standard. Ne me quitte pas in Nina's voice is devastating. The title track is pure command.
Four Women is a history of Black womanhood in four verses. Lilac Wine is heartbreak. The title track soars.
Reinvents a Randy Newman song as protest. Everything Must Change is Nina at her most reflective. A late career gem.
That tritone. The Devil's interval. Tony Iommi played it and invented a genre on a rainy day in Birmingham.
War Pigs, Iron Man, Fairies Wear Boots. Three all-time riffs on one album. Paranoid was written in five minutes as filler.
Sweet Leaf is an ode to weed. Children of the Grave is proto-thrash. Into the Void is the heaviest thing recorded in 1971.
Supernaut is the riff. Changes is the ballad nobody expected. Snowblind is about cocaine and doesn't hide it. Peak excess.
Dio joins. Neon Knights opens like a second debut. The title track is ten minutes of Dio at his most mythic.
No Woman, No Cry. Lively Up Yourself. First album with the new Wailers lineup. Revolution as a love letter.
Jamming, Three Little Birds, One Love — all one album. Natural Mystic opens like a prayer. Time called it album of the century.
The gentle one. Is This Love, Sun Is Shining, Easy Skanking. Love songs from a revolutionary on a day off.
Zimbabwe became an actual anthem for an actual revolution. Africa Unite, Wake Up and Live. The most political Marley.
Redemption Song is acoustic guitar and Bob's voice. Nothing else. Could You Be Loved is disco-reggae. His final studio album.
Southern Man lit a fuse. Only Love Can Break Your Heart is Neil at his gentlest. The title track is a fever dream.
Heart of Gold went number one and Neil spent decades running from it. Old Man and Needle and the Damage Done are devastating.
Recorded drunk, mourning two friends lost to heroin. The darkest album of the 70s. Raw and intentionally ugly.
Revolution Blues imagines the Manson Family. Ambulance Blues is eight minutes of scorched earth. Neil at his most bleak.
Acoustic side, electric side. Powderfinger might be his greatest song. My My, Hey Hey predicted punk.
Your Song. That's it. That's the entry point. Border Song and The King Must Die prove the range was there from day one.
Tiny Dancer took 30 years to become the anthem it deserved to be. Levon is a family epic in four minutes.
Double album, zero filler. Bennie and the Jets, Candle in the Wind, Saturday Night's Alright. The Taupin-John partnership at its peak.

album_title: Captain Fantastic and the Brown Dirt Cowboy
description: First album to debut at number one. Someone Saved My Life Tonight is autobiography as epic. The whole thing plays like a novel.
The comeback. I'm Still Standing is defiance as pop. I Guess That's Why They Call It the Blues brought Stevie Wonder on harmonica.
Wuthering Heights — written at 18, released at 19. The Man With the Child in His Eyes recorded at 16. Arrived from another planet.
Suspended in Gaffa is a fever. The title track is unhinged percussion and accents. Her label hated it. She was right.
Running Up That Hill on side one. The Ninth Wave suite on side two — a woman drowning, dreaming, surviving. Perfect front to back.
Twelve years of silence, then this. Pi is literally about the number. A Coral Room is about loss. An album that breathes.
Seven songs, each one long and patient. Snowflake floats. Lake Tahoe haunts. Stephen Fry counts to fifty. Winter as art.
Where Is My Mind? became the sound of a generation's breakdown. Bone Machine and Gigantic hit like trucks. Steve Albini recorded it raw.
Debaser opens with a scream about Un Chien Andalou. Monkey Gone to Heaven is ecology as noise pop. Here Comes Your Man is the secret pop song.
Space and surf. Velouria is gorgeous. Dig for Fire grooves. The most melodic Pixies record — still weird, just sunnier.
Michael Stipe mumbles and it's beautiful. Radio Free Europe, Talk About the Passion. College rock's founding document.
It's the End of the World as We Know It is the song everyone knows the chorus to. The One I Love broke them into the mainstream.
Everybody Hurts, Man on the Moon, Nightswimming. Three songs that define melancholy. Drive opens like a whisper and the album never shouts.
Losing My Religion — a mandolin riff that became inescapable. Shiny Happy People is the outlier. Country Feedback is the masterpiece.
Recorded on the road, sounds like it. E-Bow the Letter with Patti Smith. Electrolite closes the book on one era.
Unfinished Sympathy is the greatest trip-hop song ever recorded. Daydreaming and Safe From Harm set the template. Bristol invented a genre.
Teardrop became the House theme. Angel is seven minutes of dread. Dissolved Girl soundtracked The Matrix. Dark, paranoid, essential.
Everything But the Girl's Tracey Thorn sings the title track. Karmacoma creeps. Sly is a slow burn. The band finding its shadows.
Paradise Circus is a torch song from the abyss. Splitting the Atom is skeletal. Eight years in the making, sounds like controlled tension.
Solitude Is Bliss is the mission statement. Alter Ego swirls. Expectation builds for seven minutes then detonates. Psych rock from a bedroom in Perth.
Feels Like We Only Go Backwards is a pop song disguised as a spiral. Elephant stomps. Apocalypse Dreams melts and reforms. The leap.
Let It Happen is eight minutes of surrendering to change. The Less I Know the Better is the bassline everyone stole. New Person, Same Old Mistakes closed a chapter.
Posthumous Forgiveness is Kevin Parker talking to his dead father. Breathe Deeper grooves. On Track is quiet devastation disguised as synth pop.
Best Rapper Alive wasn't a boast, it was a fact. Fireman and Fly In don't let up. The moment Wayne went from hot to untouchable.
A Milli is a loop and a voice and nothing else. Lollipop went number one. Mr. Carter and Dr. Carter are Wayne at his sharpest. Went nuclear.
6 Foot 7 Foot is Wayne at maximum Weezy. She Will grooves. Nightmares of the Bottom is the deep cut. The album everyone was gonna hate but couldn't.
Don't Cry opens with X. Mona Lisa with Kendrick is a movie. Dedicate and Uproar prove six years away didn't dull anything.
Umbrella changed her entire trajectory. Don't Stop the Music and Shut Up and Drive kept the energy. The reinvention album.
Only Girl, What's My Name, S&M, Man Down, California King Bed. Five completely different singles and they all worked.
Work is a dancehall earworm. Kiss It Better is rock Rihanna. Needed Me is cold. The album she always wanted to make.
In Da Club, Many Men, 21 Questions. Three songs that defined 2003. Patiently Waiting with Em is the bonus. Bulletproof debut.
Candy Shop and Outta Control ran the radio. Piggy Bank was the diss track everyone talked about. Second round, still swinging.
I Used to Love H.E.R. is hip hop as a woman you're losing. The title track samples Ahmad Jamal. Chicago's Illmatic.
The Light is the greatest hip hop love song. The Questions and Dooinit hit different. J Dilla production throughout. Flawless.
Kanye produced the whole thing. The Corner, GO!, Faithful. Every track belongs. Short, no filler, all soul.
Kick Push is a skateboard anthem that's really about persistence. Daydreamin' and The Instrumental are Lupe at his densest. A debut with no wasted bars.
Superstar crossed over. Paris Tokyo grooves. The Coolest and Hip Hop Saved My Life are concept tracks that actually land.
Mural opens with seven minutes of unbroken bars. Dots & Lines and Prisoner 1 & 2 are layered deep. The comeback album that was fire doe.
Icy started everything. That's My Hood is a mission statement. The blueprint for trap before anyone called it that.
Lemonade and Heavy were radio Gucci. Spotlight and Wasted were club Gucci. The album that proved trap could go platinum.
First album after prison. Guwop Home is triumphant. Pop Music is Gucci being self-aware. A whole new Gucci, same ice.
Clint Eastwood is hip hop through a cartoon filter. Tomorrow Comes Today and 5/4 are Britpop's ghost. A debut that shouldn't work but does.
Feel Good Inc. is the bassline and De La Soul. DARE is the earworm. Every Planet We Reach Is Dead is the heartbreak. Dark, playful, huge.
On Melancholy Hill is pure beauty. Empire Ants builds from whisper to anthem. Stylo with Bobby Womack and Mos Def is a car chase. The ambitious one.
Pump It Up was the club hit. The rest of the album is darker, realer. Focus and Survivor show what Budden does when the cameras leave.
Broke and Immortal are peak Budden introspection. The production is moody throughout. The album for when you need a good rant.
Hard in da Paint is exactly what it sounds like. No Hands ran every party. O Let's Do It started a movement. Pure energy, zero apologies.
3005 is the hit. The Library and Sweatpants are the deep cuts. A concept album about the internet that actually works. Feel dirty for liking it.
Redbone is a Funkadelic dream. Me and Your Mama opens with a scream. Terrified is Prince-level falsetto. The pivot nobody expected.
